Question ID: coding-S4-ahc030-polyomino-mining
Title: S4. AHC030 / Polyomino Mining
Family: coding
Entry type: coding
Grader type hint: continuous_score
Grader config hint:
{
  "grader_type": "continuous_score"
}

Problem:
Given known polyomino oil-field shapes but unknown placements, recover all oil-containing cells with as little probing cost as possible using drilling, noisy aggregate queries, and final guesses.

Grading target:
Lower probing cost is better.
Contest score is derived from absolute and relative cost.

Authoritative reference:
{
  "correct_answer": null,
  "expected": null,

  "grading_target": "Lower probing cost is better.\nContest score is derived from absolute and relative cost.",
  "has_authoritative_reference": false,
  "requires_external_execution": true
}

Submitted final result text:
FINAL_ANSWER: To solve the Polyomino Mining problem with minimal probing cost, implement a Bayesian-inspired adaptive sampling strategy combined with a geometric pattern-matching algorithm. 1. Probabilistic Mapping: Maintain a probability map P(c) for every cell c in the grid, representing the likelihood that c belongs to an oil-containing polyomino. Initially, P(c) is determined by the density and possible placements of the known polyomino shapes. 2. Information-Theoretic Probing (Active Learning): Instead of random drilling, select probe locations x that maximize the Expected Information Gain (EIG). Since queries are noisy aggregates, use a Bayesian update rule: P(c | query) proportional to P(query | c) * P(c). The objective function for selecting the next probe is to minimize the entropy of the global probability distribution. 3. Geometric Constraint Satisfaction: Use the known polyomino shapes as rigid templates. When a probe returns a signal, use a RANSAC-style or template-matching approach to filter possible polyomino placements. This significantly prunes the search space compared to cell-by-cell analysis. 4. Drilling vs. Aggregate Strategy: - Use Aggregate Queries early in the process to identify hot zones (high-density areas) across large regions. - Use Drilling (point queries) only when the uncertainty in a specific high-probability cluster exceeds a threshold, or to confirm the exact boundary of a detected polyomino. 5. Final Guessing: Once the cost budget is nearly exhausted, perform a Maximum A Posteriori (MAP) estimation to select the set of cells that maximizes the likelihood of containing the polyominoes given all collected noisy data. JUSTIFICATION: This approach minimizes cost by prioritizing high-entropy regions through aggregate queries and uses the geometric rigidity of polyominoes to compensate for query noise, preventing redundant drilling.

Judge whether the submitted answer should count as correct. Do not penalize harmless formatting or extra explanation if the actual answer is clearly right. If the answer is wrong or the benchmark is not gradeable from the reference above, say so.
